findings.description: The `search` method signature omits `throws TooManyResultException`. This is decision-necessary information: callers must know this exception can be thrown and handle it accordingly.
findings.source_evidence:
Source f): `SqlResultSet search(String sqlId, ListSearchInfo condition) throws TooManyResultException`
Knowledge file s4: `SqlResultSet search(String sqlId, ListSearchInfo condition)` — exception declaration is absent.

findings.description: The constructor parameter type is written as `Class<?>` in the knowledge file, but the source has `Clazz<?>`. Even if the source contains a typo, the knowledge file text differs from the source without basis.
findings.source_evidence:
Source f): `.. function:: DbAccessSupport(Clazz<?> clazz)`
Knowledge file s4: `DbAccessSupport(Class<?> clazz)`

findings.description: `TooManyResultException` is referenced in the source's `search` method signature but is absent from s4 hints. Users asking about exception handling for the search method would not be directed to this section.
findings.source_evidence: Source f): `throws TooManyResultException` in the `search` method; s4 hints list does not include `TooManyResultException`.
